_comment: Signal-based variant assignment. No pre-defined segments — segments emerge from data.

digital_awareness.description: How digitally aware is this business?

indicators.high: 50+ Google photos, hours/menu filled, social media linked, responds to reviews online
indicators.medium: 10-50 photos, basic listing info, may have dormant social media
indicators.low: < 10 photos, incomplete listing, no social media

brand_pride.description: How much does the owner care about how the business is presented?

indicators.high: Consistent logo/colors, professional signage, branded menu, clean storefront
indicators.medium: Some branding effort, mix of professional and informal
indicators.low: Handwritten signs, no consistent branding, utilitarian presentation

review_responsive.description: Does the owner/manager reply to Google reviews?

indicators.true: Replies to reviews (especially negative ones), personalized responses
indicators.false: No replies, or only generic auto-replies

growth_phase.description: Is the business in active growth mode?

indicators.true: Newer business (< 3 years) with rapid review growth, expanding, recently renovated
indicators.false: Established, steady, not visibly investing in growth

name_language.description: What language is the business name in?
name_language.note: May indicate cultural identity, target audience, and communication preferences

variant_hints._comment: Initial hypotheses for signal → variant mapping. Will be overridden by data.

_exploration_note: 30% of sends try a random variant regardless of signals. Decreases to 10% after 40 sends.
